commit_scope: Expose Rust kernel source-compiled workload execution for BML-authored Form tending cells.

commands: cd form/form-kernel-rust && cargo fmt --check && cargo build --release
local_validation.summary: The Rust kernel now has a `run --stdlib <dir> <file.fk...>` binding that lowers section-authored Form/BML files through the existing Form source compiler and executes the resulting Form object directly. `make native-route-goal-tending` no longer uses the stale raw Rust parse path; it executes the route-goal cells through the native source-aware workload runner.

observed_delta.previous_failure: make native-route-goal-tending failed with `form-kernel-rust: parse error ... got STRING "NATIVE-ROUTE-OBS"` because the target handed BML source directly to the raw S-expression reader.
observed_delta.new_native_command: form-kernel-rust run --stdlib form-stdlib form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/kernel-http.fk form-stdlib/native-route-goal-cells.fk form-stdlib/queries/native-route-goal-tending.fk

known_limitations.observed: The new Rust binding reuses the existing Form source compiler and stdlib bootstrap. It does not replace the source compiler itself; it exposes the missing native workload entry so tending commands do not fall back to raw parse or Python glue.

deploy_validation.notes: No public endpoint behavior changes. Post-merge deploy validation should confirm production remains aligned.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: A BML-authored Form tending workload that previously failed in the Rust CLI now runs natively through the Rust kernel's source-compiled workload path and returns the native route-goal attention object.
test_flows: Run `make native-route-goal-tending` and observe a NATIVE-ROUTE-GOAL value instead of a raw parser error.
Run the targeted Rust test `source_compiled_workload_executes_bml_tending_cells` to prove the binding over the real route-goal cell files.

phase_gate.reason: Local proof is complete. CI, merge, and post-deploy observation remain before claiming release.
